lenses.generalist: Search broadly for the simplest exact generative rule. Compare several plausible structures before committing.
lenses.replicate_minimum_description: Prefer the shortest exact rule that predicts held-out suffix terms. Penalize extra phases, exceptions, and unconstrained parameters.
lenses.replicate_hypothesis_competition: Construct at least three materially different hypotheses internally, falsify them on the prefix, and return only the strongest survivor.
lenses.replicate_block_cartographer: Look for contiguous blocks whose lengths, starts, steps, or internal shapes change systematically.
lenses.replicate_piecewise_blocks: Search for growing, shrinking, or repeating blocks with systematic boundaries and within-block arithmetic.
lenses.replicate_change_point: Test whether one compact regime changes into another at a principled boundary. Reject arbitrary one-off exceptions.
lenses.mdl_suffix_holdout: Rank candidate rules by exact suffix prediction first and description length second. Refit the winner on the full prefix only after it predicts a hidden suffix.
lenses.tournament_with_suffix: Generate a direct formula, a recurrence, a phased or interwoven rule, and a block procedure when plausible. Eliminate each with a suffix holdout and full replay.
lenses.block_boundary_table: Mark every plausible boundary and tabulate each segment's length, first value, last value, and within-segment increments. Extrapolate the boundary laws, not the final segment alone.
lenses.block_start_step_table: Test whether consecutive segments have systematic lengths and arithmetic interiors whose starting values and steps follow their own low-degree trends.
lenses.periodic_delta_table: Write first and higher differences in rows by candidate phase. Look for a simple polynomial evolution down each phase column and verify a late cycle.
lenses.lagged_difference_table: For several small lags, compare each term with the prior term in the same residue class. Model those lagged increments by cycle index and verify the last available transition.
lenses.stream_alignment: Try several offsets and small stream counts rather than assuming odd/even alignment. Score each alignment by how simply every extracted stream continues.
lenses.recurrence_residual_hybrid: Fit a low-order recurrence, inspect its residual sequence, and test whether the residual follows a compact phase or polynomial law. Require both layers to replay the prefix.
lenses.local_global_decomposition: Separate global growth from local oscillation, phase, or block position. Model both parts compactly and recombine them for the continuation.
lenses.structural_triage: First compare direct formulas, recurrences, interwoven streams, phased differences, bounded wraparound, and variable-length blocks as open possibilities. Then solve only the best-supported structure exactly.
lenses.modular_affine_reconstruction: If values stay bounded while ordinary multiplication would escape, test affine updates followed by wraparound. Infer multiplier, offset, and modulus from repeated transitions and replay every wrap.
lenses.modulus_from_residuals: For a candidate affine update, compute mentally how far each raw prediction is from the observed next value. A genuine modulus must divide all nonzero residuals and also explain non-wrapping transitions.
lenses.bounded_wrap_detector: Treat sharp drops in an otherwise multiplicative or affine process as possible wraps. Search for one bound just above the observed range that makes all transitions consistent.
lenses.phase_dependent_modular: Test bounded recurrences whose multiplier or offset alternates by a short phase. Compare transitions only within the same phase and require one common modulus.
lenses.growing_length_blocks: Explicitly test consecutive block lengths such as 1,2,3,... or another simple progression. Within each candidate block, check arithmetic or polynomial structure and predict across the next boundary.
lenses.boundary_or_interleave: When two explanations seem possible, compare a variable-length block parsing with fixed-position interwoven streams. Use the whole-prefix compression and suffix prediction to decide.
lenses.lag_class_polynomials: Group positions by a small lag and build finite differences separately within each class. Extrapolate every class by its cycle index before restoring original order.
lenses.interwoven_mixed_rules: Allow different extracted streams to use different simple laws, such as one recurrence and one polynomial. Reject any stream model that needs arbitrary corrections.
lenses.phase_polynomial_deltas: Assume the update phase repeats but each phase's increment may evolve polynomially across cycles. Fit phase columns independently and verify the last complete cycle.
lenses.exact_segmenter: Search segmentations systematically. Favor boundaries that make several consecutive segments obey the same compact rule for length, start, step, or internal shape.
lenses.internal_three_experts: Independently form three candidate continuations using algebraic, recursive, and procedural views. Cross-check them on hidden suffix terms and return the surviving answer.
lenses.counterfactual_suffix: For each leading rule, ask what it would have predicted one, two, and three visible terms ago. Reject rules discovered only from the final few values.
lenses.minimal_program_simulator: Describe the shortest small program that emits one term at a time without exceptions. Mentally run it through the entire prefix and then exactly five more steps.
lenses.wildcard_compression: Seek an unusual compact construction outside the obvious trend, but demand better whole-prefix compression and suffix prediction than the simplest conventional alternative.
